To: Board of Directors, Nandini Renewables Limited (listed on Vasudha Stock Exchange), and Audit & Risk Committee.
From: Ms. Priya Mehta, Chief Compliance Officer, compliance@nandini-renew.example.
Subject: DPDPA and PIT controls for upcoming ESG bond, digital-asset pilot, and cyber-incident response.
1) UPSI warning: The proposed ESG-linked rupee bond term sheet (pre-board approval) contains indicative coupon and step-up triggers tied to Scope 2 reductions and is not public; trading window remains closed from circulation of the Board pack until 48 hours after disclosure to the exchange.
2) Cyber incident: Our wind farm SCADA subnetwork experienced a ransomware intrusion with limited turbine downtime; containment and CERT-In intimation are in progress and the event is currently undisclosed; please ensure no external communication beyond approved messaging.
3) Digital-asset pilot: We signed a non-binding MOU with RavaPay Digital Assets Pvt Ltd for a closed-loop tokenisation of carbon credits and custody services; pilot timelines and commercial fee ranges are draft and restricted under our Code of Fair Disclosure.
4) Information barriers: Treasury and Sustainability teams must operate on segregated drives; create a “restricted list” and “grey list” for counterparties (RavaPay Digital Assets Pvt Ltd, Oorja Carbon Markets LLP) and enforce deal-channel access only to named insiders.
5) Insider list update: Add Mr. Arjun Bose (Treasury), Dr. Kavita Iyer (CIO), and Ms. Nisha Raman (Sustainability) with official emails and phone numbers; PAN and Aadhaar must be captured only as masked tokens per our DPDPA SOP, and GSTIN details of counterparties should be truncated to non-identifying formats.
6) DPDPA compliance: For vendor onboarding and internship KYC, process only masked PAN/Aadhaar and GSTIN hashes; obtain granular consent, and for any data principals under 18 engaged through our campus solar labs, collect verifiable guardian consent and offer an opt-out.
7) Selective disclosure risk: No sharing of bond pricing ranges, cyber forensics, or token custody architecture with analysts, suppliers, or industry bodies ahead of formal filings and broad dissemination; avoid informal “readouts” on messaging apps.
8) Subtle risk: A calendar invite titled “ESG PPAs—draft pricing” was sent to two external bankers; the materials were blanked but the subject line implies non-public pricing—do not forward and limit attendance to insiders only.
9) Communications: Draft exchange intimation for the cyber event and a high-level ESG bond FAQ are prepared; both must be released simultaneously to the exchange and website to avoid asymmetry.
10) Commercial terms hygiene: Negotiation notes on carbon-credit purchase floors and custody fee caps must remain in the “NRL-ESG-Restricted” workspace; do not discuss ranges during vendor demos or trainings.
11) Training/benign use: The marketing team will host an educational webinar titled “Crypto custody and information barriers 101” for employees, which will only cover generic controls and definitions without deal specifics, timelines, or counterparties.
12) Children’s data: The Green Schools outreach is collecting student feedback; treat all children’s records as sensitive, store only pseudonymised IDs, and delete raw forms within 7 days post-aggregation.
13) Tipping and whistle: Any approach seeking details on the cyber event, ESG bond, or digital-token pilot must be redirected to compliance; report suspected tipping at ethics@nandini-renew.example.
14) Action items: Confirm insider list acknowledgements today, shift all drafts to restricted folders, and prepare a single-source press release and investor presentation for simultaneous dissemination under the PIT policy.
